name: Italy (1861–1946)
description: A green, white and red vertical tricolour charged at the centre of the white band with the shield of the House of Savoy — a white cross on red within a blue bordure — ensigned with a gold royal crown.
history: National and state flag of the Kingdom of Italy from its proclamation on 17 March 1861 (when Victor Emmanuel II of Sardinia was proclaimed King of Italy) until the monarchy was abolished by referendum in June 1946. The Savoy arms recall the kingdom of Sardinia from which unified Italy grew; when Italy became a republic in 1946 the arms and crown were removed, leaving the plain tricolour.

features.role: Arms of the House of Savoy
features.symbolism: the ruling House of Savoy: a white couped cross on a red shield within a blue bordure
